page: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine belonging to the DDCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz with a single physical core and one thread per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage consists of a bundled 77 GB disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data highlights a clear distinction between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance of 1879.39 and Geekbench single-core score of 1626.0 both rank in the average tier. However, due to the single-vCPU limitation, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core of 1879.39 and Geekbench multi-core of 1645.0 fall into the poor tier. Memory performance is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 13.71 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 11.19 GB/sec, both placing in the bottom 10%. Cryptographic and compression tasks show moderate capability, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 28.36 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 10.36 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC1ds_v3 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 for a low-resource instance, making it suitable for workloads requiring more memory than compute power without paying for unneeded CPU cores. However, the single-core bottleneck and low memory bandwidth limit its utility for parallel processing, heavy database operations, or high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, small-scale LLM text generation, or low-demand utility workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and a 77 GB local disk.
description: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with one physical core and one thread. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, a 77 GB local storage disk, and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1626.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1879.39. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the bottom 10% due to the single-vCPU constraint and a 13.71 GB/sec large-block read speed. This instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 for low-compute requirements. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, small-scale utility tasks, and low-demand workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high parallel processing power or intensive memory bandwidth.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine featuring a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight, memory-leaning workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-vCPU instance with an Intel Xeon 8370C CPU, 8.0 GB RAM, and 77 GB local storage for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A single-core virtual machine featuring a dedicated processor, high memory-to-core ratio, and local storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 1 vCPU and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
77 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic text processing
